title: Nephrodium auriculatum

If you're interested in learning about a similar fern, here's a paragraph on Nephrolepis auriculata:

Nephrolepis auriculata, also known as the auriculated sword fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to tropical regions. The health benefits of Nephrolepis auriculata include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites that contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Nephrolepis auriculata is often used to prepare tea, infusions, and tinctures that are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and skin problems.
taxonomy: Nephrodium auriculatum is classified within the family Matoniaceae, a group of ancient ferns. It belongs to the order Matoniales, which is a distinct order of ferns that was previously considered part of the Polypodiales. The genus Nephrodium is often considered a synonym of the genus Matonia. However, some classifications still recognize Nephrodium as a separate genus. Further research is needed to determine the most accurate classification of Nephrodium auriculatum.
